opp.name: Chantier Davie Canada OppS2C - Jan'25
opp.next_step: 6/16MF - account transitioned to MikeF. Intro EM sent to Rachel VP Supply Chain re: project status. Sounding as though Oracle bias is a blocker but confirming. 12/10 RH MM connected with Rachel. Update: "This process has been extensive, and it seems time is not in our favour in many aspects due to the parallel Oracle implementation we are navigating, we are now on phase 3. We are having several discussions internally lately and will likely come to a decision to collapse the RFP activities due to competing priorities, which is creating capacity bottleneck. This is not an official note, but rather an indication on where I see these things going as of now… We have a meeting scheduled for the last week of October and will know better where we are going with this and will then provide a formal notification on what we will do next. Again, we apologize for the lengthy process, and we are committed to provide an update on what is next as soon as we reach an agreement on the way forward.
opp.ais_why:
• The opportunity description and discovery notes both state that Davie is "exploring Zycus' Gen AI capabilities, particularly in sourcing and vendor management". That buyer-stated interest is on record. The Feb 13 2025 demo notes confirm Zycus showed generative AI capabilities for RFP creation and supplier response evaluation, and Maria from legal engaged on CLM AI features.
• Merlin and Agentic AI are included in Products, but ANA and Merlin Intake are absent. AI importance is rated 'Important' rather than 'Very Critical'. The buyer's questions concentrated on Oracle integration, data residency in Canada and out-of-the-box fit. The AI thread was real but not the lead requirement.
• The deal has stalled. A December 2024 update notes Davie is likely to collapse the RFP activities because of competing priorities, with a parallel Oracle Fusion implementation taking precedence. The AI interest exists, but the capacity bottleneck and ERP priorities are overshadowing it, so the score stays on the Curious side of the line.

items.quote: it's so manual, that's far beyond from where we wanna be

items.area: Outcome of the RFP decision communicated to proponents
items.quote: we had issued communication to all Proponents that participated in the RFP last year with results and our decision on the way forward

items.area: Decision maker / procurement director identity not on Salesforce contact roles (named only on calls)

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists. Across the discovery workshop (03 Feb 2025) and the clarification session (11 Apr 2025) the buyer articulated only qualitative pain: Rachel Lemos said on 11 Apr 2025 'it's so manual, that's far beyond from where we wanna be' and 'we're far away from where we want to be.' No savings target, headcount reduction, cycle-time, or ROI figure was ever stated. The absence of a financial justification makes it far easier for the buyer to defer procurement into the Oracle implementation. Quantifying the cost of the manual status quo (and the cost of waiting) is the move and should anchor any re-engagement.

sources: Clarification session 11 Apr 2025 (Jean-Philippe Ouellet-Larochelle, Rachel Lemos)
champion.narrative: Jean-Philippe Ouellet-Larochelle, Project Procurement Manager, was the developing champion: he drove demo scheduling and logistics and called the project 'dear to my heart as this will help to improve our actual process' (11 Apr 2025). Rachel Lemos was an enthusiastic early sponsor. What has NOT developed: neither has driven the deal forward since April 2025, and Rachel's December note shifted the tone toward collapse. There is no active internal advocate countering the Oracle default, and the June 2026 email routes the project's status question back through JP. Re-securing JP as the advocate who fights for a standalone S2C decision against the ERP bundle ranks above any commercial step.

sources: SF Next_Step__c owner note dated 16 Jun
competition.narrative: The competition is not a rival S2P vendor in an active bake-off, it is Oracle and inertia. Davie uses Oracle for services and is mid-implementation of Oracle Fusion (Rachel Lemos, 13 Feb 2025). By 10 Dec 2025 the Oracle phase-3 program had become the competing priority threatening to collapse the standalone RFP, and the owner's 16 Jun 2026 note reads 'sounding as though Oracle bias is a blocker but confirming.' The risk is Davie adopting Oracle's native procurement modules by default rather than choosing Zycus. We win by separating deep sourcing / contract-lifecycle depth and Merlin AI from ERP transactional procurement, and by activating the Canadian-government data-residency / compliance requirement as a forcing function Oracle native may not satisfy as cleanly.

sources: Clarification session 11 Apr 2025 (Jean-Philippe Ouellet-Larochelle, Rachel Lemos)
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly articulated and confirmed. Sourcing runs through email with no formal process, and vendor management is manual: 'the vendor management process is mostly done manually... through email' and results in the 'bad habit to always go with the same vendors' (Jean-Philippe Ouellet-Larochelle, 11 Apr 2025). Rachel Lemos called the current state 'so manual, that's far beyond from where we wanna be' and described a 'dirty database' of supplier registrations worked monthly by hand. Legal wants contract authoring and lifecycle visibility (Maria Dinu, 13 Feb 2025). Pain owners are supply chain (Rachel / JP) and legal (Maria). The pain is real but undermined by no quantification and the competing Oracle program.

sources: Clarification session 11 Apr 2025 (Richard Hunsinger, Jean-Philippe Ouellet-Larochelle)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are partly visible. The evaluation is under NDA (Richard Hunsinger confirmed 'we're under NDA' on 11 Apr 2025), and ~90% of Davie's work is Canadian federal government, imposing Canadian-content and security-clearance requirements plus an NDA gate before RFP content access. Two unresolved paper items remain: Canadian data-residency confirmation (owned by the relevant stakeholder Citone, pending since 11 Apr 2025) and AI/data-security assurance. No MSA / SOW / redline stage has begun. What would create visibility is the formal decision Rachel promised plus a written data-residency answer.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Rachel Lemos, VP of Supply Chain)
economic_buyer.narrative: Rachel Lemos, VP of Supply Chain, is the economic buyer and controlled the framing throughout: on 13 Feb 2025 she set scope ('what we're looking for... is to automate the sourcing process') and confirmed Procure-to-Pay out of scope. She was an enthusiastic sponsor early ('we're very excited about this project... decision made very soon', 11 Apr 2025) but her 10 Dec 2025 note shifted to signalling a probable RFP collapse. Her budget authority is clear from Salesforce (VP of Supply Chain, primary buyer contact), but her buy-in is now cooling and no commercial or pricing conversation has ever taken place. Re-securing her active sponsorship, not merely identifying her, is the open item.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal RFP-led evaluation: NDA gate before RFP content access, cross-functional sign-off on RFP sections (JP described interfunctional approval flows, 11 Apr 2025), and a demo cohort spanning supply chain, legal and VP IT. Rachel set a decision checkpoint for the last week of October and promised a formal notification afterward (SF Next_Step__c). That process is now colliding with the Oracle Fusion phase-3 rollout, which the buyer says is creating a capacity bottleneck that may collapse the RFP. The June 2026 email that a decision on the way forward was already communicated to RFP proponents means the process may have concluded without our visibility, which the deal team must confirm.

sources: Demo workshop 13 Feb 2025 (Rachel Lemos, Maria Dinu, Michael)
decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well documented from the demo. The buyer evaluated out-of-the-box fit ('a solution that has a lot of out of the box functionalities that we don't need to be customizing', Rachel Lemos 13 Feb 2025), Oracle Fusion compatibility, contract-lifecycle authoring for legal (Maria Dinu: 'a full contract life cycle management software'), gating so suppliers accept NDAs before RFP access (Michael, 13 Feb 2025), and mandatory-versus-scored questions with automatic disqualification (Rachel Lemos, 13 Feb 2025). Canadian data residency for federal-government content is a hard criterion (Richard Hunsinger raised it 11 Apr 2025, pending VP IT confirmation). AI is 'important' but not the lead requirement per the AIS read.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Formal Evaluation' anchors near 35. Thin signals pull the read to 28.
blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

The June status email again routes through him, which is an opening to re-activate him.

cro_panel.header: Chantier Davie Canada — Formal Evaluation · $225K · closes 14 Oct

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 28 is carried by champion strength +0.30 (weight 15); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 54 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 12 is anchored on the stage win (28), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 54, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 5.0 / Moderate. Calls confirm genuine appetite ('there is appetite for us to streamline and use AI, without the risks', Rachel Lemos 11 Apr 2025) but gated on data-security for government data, and AI is secondary to Oracle-fit and out-of-box requirements. Field read and call read agree: curious, not hungry.

items.change: Account ownership transitioned to Mike Flowers
Buyer status email: decision on the way forward already communicated to last year's RFP proponents; project status routed back through Jean-Philippe
Close date pushed 2026-08-30 to 2026-10-14 (fifth slip, no stage advance)
Buyer signalled probable RFP collapse due to Oracle phase-3 capacity bottleneck

deal_movement.summary: Real progression stopped after the April 2025 demo. The 2025 field history is repeated close-date pushes (2025-11-20 to 2026-03-20 to 2026-04-30 to 2026-08-30 to 2026-10-14) with no stage advance. The 10 Dec 2025 note warned of RFP collapse under Oracle. The account moved to Mike Flowers on 2026-06-11, and the only 2026 buyer touch is a status email on 2026-06-16/17.

In June Rachel said a decision on the way forward had already been communicated to last year's RFP proponents, so an award decision may already be made.
The account transitioned to owner Mike Flowers in June 2026 and the buyer relationship is being rebuilt from a cold status check, not active engagement.
No pricing or ROI case has ever landed and the manual-process pain was never quantified, so there is no dollar argument against deferring to Oracle.

items: 14 months since the last live buyer working session (April 2025 demo)
Buyer note warns of probable RFP collapse (10 Dec 2025)
Only 2026 touch is a rep-initiated status email (16-17 Jun 2026)
confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is cold. The only 2026 buyer contact is a status email after the owner transition; real working sessions stopped in April 2025 and the buyer is signalling a decision may already be made.

north_star_verdict.math: Close 2026-10-14 is 103 days out. Created 2025-01-29, so ~17 months in cycle and still in Formal Evaluation. Last real working session was the demo in April 2025 (~14 months ago). A buyer email reply landed 2026-06-17 but only as a status exchange after the owner transition, not deal progression. The 10 Dec 2025 note flagged probable RFP collapse; the late-Oct 2025 decision checkpoint passed without formal notification. No movement toward Validation / Proposal / Negotiation.
evidence: SF Next_Step__c (Rachel Lemos, dated 10 Dec): buyer indicated a likely decision to collapse RFP activities due to competing Oracle priorities creating a capacity bottleneck.
SF activity 2026-06-16 (buyer email, Rachel Lemos): 'We had issued communication to all Proponents that participated in the RFP last year with results and our decision on the way forward.'
SF LastActivityDate 2026-06-17: only 2026 touch is a status email after the account transitioned to Mike Flowers on 2026-06-11.
Last recorded live buyer working session: S2C demo, April 2025 (~14 months ago).
north_star_verdict.headline: The deal has been stalled ~14 months, still in Formal Evaluation, and the buyer's own December 2025 note warned of a likely RFP collapse under a parallel Oracle Fusion phase-3 rollout. The only 2026 contact is a June status email after the account moved to Mike Flowers, and Rachel says a decision on the way forward was already communicated to last year's RFP proponents, so an award may already have been made.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (or Omitted): Best Case is indefensible with no quantified business case, a cooling economic buyer, an Oracle-default blocker, and a buyer-signalled probable RFP collapse.

flags: Confirm the RFP is still alive before any selling: Rachel says a decision was already communicated to proponents, so pin down whether we are in or out.
Neutralize the Oracle-default blocker: position Zycus as the layer above Oracle Fusion and bring an Oracle-integration reference to the relevant stakeholder Citone.
Build a quantified cost-of-manual and cost-of-waiting case to give Rachel a dollar argument against folding procurement into the Oracle rollout.
best_practice_check.summary: Three levers matter most: confirm the RFP is even alive, neutralize the Oracle-default blocker, and rebuild a quantified reason to buy standalone.

competitive_position.summary: Single dominant threat: Oracle. Davie is mid-implementation of Oracle Fusion, and by 10 Dec 2025 that phase-3 program had become the competing priority threatening to collapse the standalone S2C RFP; the owner's 16 Jun 2026 note flags 'Oracle bias' as a likely blocker. This is competition by inertia and default, not an active S2P bake-off. We win by differentiating deep sourcing, contract lifecycle and Merlin AI from ERP-native procurement, and by leveraging the Canadian-government data-residency and compliance requirement as a forcing function.

competitors.name: Oracle Fusion (native procurement modules)
competitors.quote: This process has been extensive, and it seems time is not in our favour in many aspects due to the parallel Oracle implementation we are navigating, we are now on phase 3... we will likely come to a decision to collapse the RFP activities due to competing priorities, which is creating capacity bottleneck.
competitors.source: SF Next_Step__c (Rachel Lemos); Demo workshop 13 Feb 2025; owner note 16 Jun 2026
competitors.how_we_win: Reframe Zycus as the best-of-breed procurement layer on top of Oracle Fusion (not a replacement): a phased post-go-live deployment requiring zero Oracle rework, using our native Oracle connectors, and delivering Merlin AI sourcing, contract authoring and government-workflow depth Oracle native cannot match; bring a Zycus-Oracle integration reference to VP IT Jeremy Citone.

competitors.name: Do nothing / defer procurement into the Oracle rollout
competitors.quote: We are having several discussions internally lately and will likely come to a decision to collapse the RFP activities due to competing priorities.
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of the manual status quo and the cost of waiting, then tie a light, phased first-scope (sourcing + CLM for legal) to the government-compliance clock so standalone S2C survives alongside the Oracle program rather than being folded into it.

items.deliverable: Get written confirmation from Jean-Philippe on whether the S2C RFP is still active or collapsed

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI was demonstrated (Merlin generative RFP question generation, Teams intake, contract OCR) and drew visible buyer excitement, but AI is not the lead requirement: buyer questions concentrated on Oracle integration, data residency and out-of-box fit, so the Merlin / Agentic AI story is real but under-leveraged against the Oracle-default risk.

we're making a huge commitment to the Canadian marketplace, and that won't be a showstopper for us either way

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Functional fit was strong on the demo (out-of-box sourcing, CLM authoring, gating/NDA, disqualification logic, Excel import, Oracle connectors), but the two exposure points are Oracle-compatibility timing and Canadian data residency.

• The opportunity description and discovery notes both state that Davie is "exploring Zycus' Gen AI capabilities, particularly in sourcing and vendor management". That buyer-stated interest is on record. The Feb 13 2025 demo notes confirm Zycus showed generative AI capabilities for RFP creation and supplier response evaluation, and Maria from legal engaged on CLM AI features.
• Merlin and Agentic AI are included in Products, but ANA and Merlin Intake are absent. AI importance is rated 'Important' rather than 'Very Critical'. The buyer's questions concentrated on Oracle integration, data residency in Canada and out-of-the-box fit. The AI thread was real but not the lead requirement.
• The deal has stalled. A December 2024 update notes Davie is likely to collapse the RFP activities because of competing priorities, with a parallel Oracle Fusion implementation taking precedence. The AI interest exists, but the capacity bottleneck and ERP priorities are overshadowing it, so the score stays on the Curious side of the line.

6/16MF - account transitioned to MikeF.  Intro EM sent to Rachel VP Supply Chain re: project status.  Sounding as though Oracle bias is a blocker but confirming.

12/10 RH MM connected with Rachel. Update:

Again, we apologize for the lengthy process, and we are committed to provide an update on what is next as soon as we reach an agreement on the way forward.
